name: Ensure queues are not publicly accessible
description: AWS SQS queues and Google Pub/Sub topics or subscriptions that grant public access.
body:
### Overview

This control ensures that **queue and messaging resources do not grant access to public principals**.

### What is checked

The assessment reviews AWS SQS queue policies and Google Cloud Pub/Sub IAM bindings for public access patterns, including wildcard principals, allUsers, and allAuthenticatedUsers.

### Why it matters

Public queues can allow unauthorized publishing, consuming, deleting, or inspecting messages depending on the granted permissions. That can lead to data exposure, message tampering, workflow abuse, or denial of service.

### What good looks like

Queue access should be granted only to explicit producers, consumers, service accounts, roles, or groups. Public principals should be removed, and cross-account or cross-project access should be scoped to the minimum required actions.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Remove public principals from queue and messaging policies.

### AWS

For AWS, this control identifies SQS queues when the queue policy has an allow statement for public principals.

1. Open the affected SQS queue.
2. Review **Access policy**.
3. Remove allow statements with principals such as `*`, empty principal, or `AWS|*`.
4. Grant queue actions only to specific AWS accounts, roles, or services.
5. Test producers and consumers with the explicit principals.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

```bash
aws sqs get-queue-attributes --queue-url <queue-url> --attribute-names Policy --region {{asset.region}}
```

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-basic-examples-of-iam-policies.html

The control is satisfied when the SQS policy no longer has public allow principals.

### GCP

For GCP, this control identifies Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions when IAM bindings include `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.

1. Open the affected Pub/Sub topic or subscription.
2. Review **Permissions**.
3. Remove `allUsers` and `allAuthenticatedUsers` from publisher, subscriber, viewer, or admin roles.
4. Grant roles to named service accounts or groups.
5. Test message publish or consume flows with the approved identities.
6. Re-run Cyscale sync and assessment.

```bash
gcloud pubsub topics remove-iam-policy-binding {{asset.name}} --member=allUsers --role=roles/pubsub.publisher
gcloud pubsub subscriptions remove-iam-policy-binding {{asset.name}} --member=allUsers --role=roles/pubsub.subscriber
```

Reference: https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/access-control

The control is satisfied when Pub/Sub IAM bindings no longer include public members.
